name: Western Australia (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the crosses of the Union Jack
the gold disc bearing the state badge
the native black swan, the state emblem

description: A blue British ensign with the Union Jack in the canton and a yellow disc in the fly bearing a black swan swimming towards the hoist.
history: Adopted in 1870 with the swan facing the fly; reversed in 1953 so the swan faces the hoist (in line with vexillological convention that animate charges face away from the fly). The black swan was the symbol of the Swan River Colony from its founding.

features.symbolism: Based on the British Blue Ensign, the standard format for Australian state flags.
the Union Jack, signifying historic ties to the United Kingdom

features.role: yellow disc bearing the black swan
features.symbolism: the gold disc serves as the field for the state badge

features.role: black swan swimming towards the hoist
features.symbolism: the native black swan (Cygnus atratus), state emblem of Western Australia
